danger_signals.meaning: Active combat threat requiring immediate tactical response

danger_signals.visual_form: Allied structure taking damage, visual indicators of assault
danger_signals.meaning: Mission objective at risk; failure to defend results in base loss and mission difficulty escalation

danger_signals.visual_form: Damaged landscape, debris fields from prior combat
danger_signals.meaning: Recent hostile activity; area has been contested

danger_signals.visual_form: Multiple enemy units converging or emerging from map edges
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating threat level; player must manage resource allocation

safety_signals.visual_form: Undamaged human installations with defensive systems operational
safety_signals.meaning: Secure operational area; resupply and mission briefing hub

safety_signals.visual_form: Allied Hercs in formation or nearby during mission
safety_signals.meaning: Tactical support available; reduced isolation risk

safety_signals.visual_form: Areas with no enemy presence or recent salvage collection
safety_signals.meaning: Temporarily secure zones for resource gathering

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual feedback (likely UI-based) indicating objectives met
safety_signals.meaning: Access to new equipment, weapons, or Herc chassis as reward

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Intact human bases, command structures, allied installation clusters across North America, South America, Antarctica, Asia, Australia, Europe

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Cybrid-controlled HERCs with hostile behavior patterns
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy Hercs differentiated from player and allied units through piloting behavior and tactical approach
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Moon-based strongholds; space colony forces; expanding territorial presence as campaign progresses

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Recovering human settlements, controlled territory across multiple continents with intact infrastructure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Human forces maintain defensive posture; Cybrids have been eliminated from Earth; calm operational tempo

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Initial space-based landings visible; moon becomes active battleground; territorial pressure increases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Human forces shift to offensive-defensive hybrid posture; squadmates gain experience through increased combat; mission difficulty escalates

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Moon base becomes heavily fortified visual focus; lunar terrain shows battle damage and Cybrid presence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Campaign culminates in final terminal missions against Prometheus; player must succeed or game ends

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Partial base destruction visible (some bases lost, others intact); varying degrees of structural damage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Branching mission paths determined by success level; continued gameplay despite tactical setbacks; strategic consequences modeled through mission selection

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Salvage fields from destroyed Hercs and buildings; new equipment visually integrated into player Herc
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Customization reflected in Herc chassis appearance; hardpoint-mounted weapons and gear alter silhouette and capability

environmental_storytelling.example: 3D rolling terrain shows combat-scarred landscape across multiple continents; wreckage and salvage metal indicate prior battles and ongoing war
Missions span North America, South America, Antarctica, Asia, Australia, Europe, and Luna; each region's terrain variation communicates different tactical contexts

environmental_storytelling.technique: Base structure integrity as mission context
environmental_storytelling.example: Visually intact or damaged allied installations convey mission criticality; player assignment to defend multiple bases simultaneously shows distributed threat
Progressive appearance of Cybrid forces from space colonies moving toward Earth and establishing moon bases visually communicates narrative threat escalation
Destroyed enemy Hercs leave behind visible wreckage with extractable weapons and components; accumulation of salvage demonstrates player resource accumulation progression

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Gain experience across missions; player assigns outfitting and chassis selection; respond to tactical command
Employ tactical formations; escalate threat through reinforcement waves; exhibit improved AI compared to predecessor game
Chassis properties affect armor, shield output, speed, and hardpoint availability; equipment mounting directly influences visual silhouette and tactical capability
Take damage during missions; destruction affects mission success degree; visual damage state communicates tactical consequence
Requires mission sequence success; defeat is mandatory for campaign completion; failure ends game

notes: EarthSiege 2 employs simulation paradigm to communicate world-state primarily through environmental destruction, tactical consequence visibility, and resource manifestation. The branching mission structure allows partial success states to visually persist (damaged but operational bases, incomplete objectives). Faction distinction relies on behavioral differentiation rather than explicit color-coding or sigil systems documented in available sources. The progression from Earth-based defense to Luna-focused offensive operations provides overarching narrative visual structure. Video briefings and debriefings (mentioned but not detailed in source) supplement visual communication. The game's improved 640x480 resolution and detailed 3D terrain compared to its predecessor enable more sophisticated environmental storytelling.
